text: parent child navigation in aim search results
description: in aim search, let's introduce navigation feature:  h shows list of supported aims, l shos list of supporting aims. This is relevant, because sometimes we find an aim but then want to select one of it's children.

reflections.context: Enable better navigation between related aims directly within the search results modal.
reflections.outcome: Users can now traverse up (parents) and down (children) from any search result, and common path suffixes are automatically grouped.
reflections.effectiveness: Highly effective. h/l navigation feels natural for graph traversal, and path merging significantly reduces UI clutter in deep hierarchies.
reflections.lesson: Suffix-based path merging is a robust way to handle 'multiple paths to the same node' visualization issues.

reflections.context: h/l navigation was partially implemented but had three bugs: (1) the unified handleKeydown blocked h/l from the input field (!isInput condition); (2) the items computed always returned externalResults even in navigation mode, making navigated results invisible; (3) Escape in navigation mode bubbled to the modal and closed it instead of stepping back.
reflections.outcome: Fixed all three: condition now allows h/l from input when query empty, items computed respects navigationMode, Escape stopsPropagation when navigating back. 7 new tests cover all paths. Browser test confirmed full h→l→Escape flow.
reflections.effectiveness: All three bugs were silent regressions from an earlier refactor — no existing tests caught them. The fix is small and targeted.
reflections.lesson: When consolidating separate event handlers into one, run tests for every code path the old handlers covered. Event bubbling bugs between nested components are easy to miss without an integration-style browser test.
reflections.pattern: Same pattern as prior picker work: unit tests catch logic but browser testing catches event propagation and DOM update timing bugs that unit tests mock away.

status.comment: h/l navigation works from result items and from empty input. Escape steps back through history without closing modal.
